footer.data: Data: World Bank · WHO · Open-Meteo · REST Countries · UNDP · World Happiness Report · Global Peace Index · UNODC

hero.title: FIND YOUR NEXT COUNTRY
hero.tagline: Personalised rankings for digital nomads — live data from 10 global sources.

map.countriesScored: {{count}} countries scored · click a country for details

compare.countrySubtitle: Select countries to compare across {{coreCount}} core indicators and {{aiCount}} AI indicators
compare.regionSubtitle: Compare average scores across world regions
compare.helperText: Choose countries using the checkboxes in the list, then click Compare to open the comparison view.
compare.nomadVisaSubtitle: Compare digital nomad visa programs side by side

weights.title: WEIGHTS & PREFERENCES
weights.hint: Click a group to expand and adjust its indicator weights.
weights.independentDesc: Each indicator is weighted independently on a 0–100 scale. Total weight can exceed 100. All indicators are considered when ranking countries — higher values = more important.
weights.balancedDesc: All visible indicators share 100% total weight. Increasing one indicator automatically decreases others proportionally. Perfect for comparing relative importance across categories.
weights.footerBalanced: All indicators share 100% — raising one lowers the others

visa.nomadVisaDesc: Show only countries that offer digital nomad visas or long-term remote work permits.
visa.schengenDesc: Show only countries within the Schengen Area — 29 European countries with passport-free travel between them.
visa.touristVisaDesc: Filter by the minimum visa-free or visa-on-arrival stay available for most Western passports. Countries requiring advance visa applications are excluded.

countryList.clickHint: Click on a country to view details

indicatorsPage.title: INDICATORS
indicatorsPage.subtitle: How each of the {{coreCount}} core indicators is calculated — data sources, methodology, and weighting. {{aiCount}} AI indicators are documented separately.
indicatorsPage.aiSectionNote: — off by default, enable in the weight panel

economy.name: Economy
economy.description: GDP per capita, income inequality, employment rates, and economic stability. Higher scores reflect stronger, more resilient economies with broad-based prosperity.

affordability.name: Affordability
affordability.description: Cost of living relative to local income. Covers housing, groceries, transport, and daily expenses using Numbeo and World Bank purchasing power data.

foodSecurity.name: Food Security
foodSecurity.description: Access to sufficient, safe, and nutritious food. FAO data tracks prevalence of undernourishment, food price stability, and agricultural production capacity.

healthcare.name: Healthcare
healthcare.description: Quality and accessibility of healthcare systems. WHO data covers life expectancy, hospital beds per capita, physician density, and health expenditure.

education.name: Education
education.description: Literacy rates, school enrollment, and quality of education institutions. UNESCO tracks years of schooling, tertiary enrollment, and educational attainment.

environment.name: Environment
environment.description: Air quality, water purity, biodiversity, and ecological sustainability. Yale's Environmental Performance Index scores countries across 32 indicators in 11 categories.

climate.name: Climate
climate.description: Temperature comfort, seasonal variation, and climate vulnerability. ND-GAIN measures a country's exposure to climate-related risks and its readiness to adapt.

safety.name: Safety
safety.description: Personal safety, crime rates, and political stability. The Global Peace Index from the Institute for Economics & Peace ranks 163 nations across 23 indicators.

infrastructure.name: Internet & Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: Broadband speed, mobile connectivity, digital infrastructure, and access. ITU and Speedtest data track fixed broadband penetration and median download speeds.

happiness.name: Happiness
happiness.description: Subjective wellbeing, life satisfaction, and social support. The World Happiness Report surveys 150,000+ people annually across six key life dimensions.

humanDevelopment.name: Human Development
humanDevelopment.description: UNDP's Human Development Index — a composite of life expectancy, education, and gross national income per capita, measuring long-run progress.

governance.name: Governance
governance.description: Rule of law, transparency, and government effectiveness. The World Bank's Worldwide Governance Indicators aggregate 30+ data sources on institutional quality.

englishProficiency.name: English Proficiency
englishProficiency.description: English language fluency measured by the EF English Proficiency Index — the world's largest ranking based on tests taken by 2.2 million adults in 113 countries.

digitalFreedom.name: Digital Freedom
digitalFreedom.description: Internet freedom measured by Freedom House's Freedom on the Net report — covering censorship, surveillance, content restrictions, and access to VPNs.

personalFreedom.name: Personal Freedom
personalFreedom.description: CATO Institute's Human Freedom Index personal sub-index — freedom of movement, expression, association, religion, and personal autonomy.

logistics.name: Logistics & Transport
logistics.description: World Bank Logistics Performance Index — customs efficiency, infrastructure quality, timeliness, and international shipping capability.

biodiversity.name: Biodiversity & Nature
biodiversity.description: Protected land area and forest coverage from World Bank data — access to natural parks, green spaces, and outdoor activities for nomads.

socialTolerance.name: Social Tolerance
socialTolerance.description: LGBTQ+ rights composite score — marriage equality, civil unions, anti-discrimination protections, and legal status from ILGA and Equaldex databases.

taxFriendliness.name: Tax Friendliness
taxFriendliness.description: Heritage Foundation Tax Burden score combined with tax revenue as % of GDP — lighter-tax countries score higher, relevant for nomad tax planning.

startupEnvironment.name: Startup Environment
startupEnvironment.description: Heritage Foundation Business Freedom score — ease of starting, operating, and closing a business. Covers regulatory burden and licensing requirements.

airConnectivity.name: Air Connectivity
airConnectivity.description: Air passengers carried (World Bank) and number of countries reachable via direct flights (OpenFlights) — route connectivity for frequent-flying digital nomads.

healthcareCost.name: Healthcare Cost
healthcareCost.description: Out-of-pocket health expenditure as percentage of total health spending — lower out-of-pocket costs mean more accessible and affordable healthcare.

nomadCommunity.name: Nomad & Expat Community
nomadCommunity.description: Coworking space density, established nomad hub cities, coliving availability, and expatriate community size — AI-analyzed composite from multiple nomad-focused data sources.
nomadCommunity.source: AI-analyzed (Claude) — NomadList, Coworker.com, InterNations, UN DESA

visaFriendliness.name: Visa & Stay Flexibility
visaFriendliness.description: Tourist visa duration, visa-run feasibility, e-visa availability, dedicated nomad visa programs, and paths to longer residency — specifically for US/EU passport holders.
visaFriendliness.source: AI-analyzed (Claude) — Henley Passport Index, IATA, NomadList, govt sources

costEfficiency.name: Nomad Cost-Value Ratio
costEfficiency.description: Quality of nomad life per dollar — not just cheapness. Rates coworking, internet speed, food quality, and housing standard relative to monthly cost.
costEfficiency.source: AI-analyzed (Claude) — NomadList, Numbeo, Expatistan, Speedtest, Airbnb

workLifeBalance.name: Remote Work & Lifestyle
workLifeBalance.description: Timezone overlap with US/EU clients, café work culture, outdoor recreation access, social scene vibrancy, and weekend escape proximity.

digitalReadiness.name: Digital Nomad Infra
digitalReadiness.description: Beyond basic internet — café wifi reliability, mobile data quality and eSIM support, digital payment adoption, and app ecosystem for daily nomad life.

culturalFit.name: Cultural Openness
culturalFit.description: Daily-life friendliness to foreigners, language accessibility for non-speakers, scam prevalence, and bureaucratic ease — real friction for remote workers, not tourist experience.
culturalFit.source: AI-analyzed (Claude) — InterNations, NomadList, World Bank, travel advisories

dataSourcesPage.title: DATA SOURCES
dataSourcesPage.subtitle: Transparent methodology — every score is traceable to its origin.

who.name: WHO
who.description: Global health statistics and epidemiological data

undp.name: UNDP
undp.description: Human Development Index combining health, education, and income

itu.name: ITU
itu.description: Global ICT statistics and connectivity data

iep.name: IEP / Vision of Humanity
iep.description: Global Peace Index measuring societal safety and security

worldHappiness.name: World Happiness Report
worldHappiness.description: Annual survey-based wellbeing and life satisfaction scores

ef.name: EF Education First
ef.description: World's largest ranking of English skills by country

fao.name: FAO
fao.description: Food and Agriculture Organization nutritional and security data

ndGain.name: ND-GAIN
ndGain.description: Notre Dame climate vulnerability and readiness index

nomadVisasPage.subtitle: Compare digital nomad visa programs across {{count}} countries

budget.title: FIND YOUR BUDGET FIT
budget.subtitle: Enter your monthly budget and discover which countries offer the best lifestyle for your money
budget.qualityBlendTooltip: Controls the balance between pure cost-of-living affordability and overall country quality (safety, healthcare, internet, infrastructure). At 0% only price matters; at 100% only quality matters.
budget.blendHint: Balances lowest cost vs. highest-quality destinations
budget.noResults: No countries with cost data available

aiIndicatorsPage.title: AI INDICATORS
aiIndicatorsPage.subtitle: How each of the {{count}} AI-generated composite scores is built — sub-indicators, data sources, and methodology.
aiIndicatorsPage.disclaimer: These {{count}} indicators are generated by AI (Claude Opus 4.6, Anthropic). Each score is a 0–100 composite synthesized from multiple publicly available data sources, structured datasets, and qualitative assessment. Scores are designed to be reproducible within ±5 points. They are disabled by default in the ranking and can be enabled in the weight panel. AI-generated scores may contain inaccuracies — always verify critical decisions with official sources.

nomadCommunity.name: Nomad & Expat Community
nomadCommunity.description: Measures the density and vibrancy of digital nomad and expatriate communities. Countries with established coworking ecosystems, nomad hubs, coliving spaces, and active expat networks score higher.
nomadCommunity.source: AI-analyzed (Claude Opus 4.6) — NomadList, Coworker.com, InterNations, UN DESA

visaFriendliness.name: Visa & Stay Flexibility
visaFriendliness.description: Assesses how easy it is for a digital nomad (holding a strong passport like US/EU) to enter, stay long-term, and extend their stay. Considers tourist visa duration, visa-run feasibility, e-visa availability, dedicated nomad visa programs, and paths to longer residency.
visaFriendliness.source: AI-analyzed (Claude Opus 4.6) — Henley Passport Index, IATA, NomadList, govt sources

costEfficiency.name: Nomad Cost-Value Ratio
costEfficiency.description: Not just cheapness — this measures the quality of nomad life per dollar spent. Countries where you get excellent coworking, reliable fast internet, good food, and comfortable housing at a reasonable price score highest.
costEfficiency.source: AI-analyzed (Claude Opus 4.6) — NomadList, Numbeo, Expatistan, Speedtest, Airbnb

workLifeBalance.name: Remote Work & Lifestyle
workLifeBalance.description: Evaluates how well a country supports the remote work lifestyle beyond just internet. Considers timezone overlap with major client regions (US/EU), café work culture, outdoor recreation access, social scene for nomads, and overall work-life rhythm.
workLifeBalance.source: AI-analyzed (Claude Opus 4.6) — timezone data, NomadList, TripAdvisor

digitalReadiness.name: Digital Nomad Infrastructure
digitalReadiness.description: Goes beyond basic internet penetration. Measures the digital ecosystem specifically relevant to nomads: café wifi reliability, mobile data quality and eSIM support, digital payment adoption, app ecosystem for daily life, and tech-forward city infrastructure.
digitalReadiness.source: AI-analyzed (Claude Opus 4.6) — Speedtest/Ookla, Opensignal, GSMA, Airalo

culturalFit.name: Cultural Openness to Foreigners
culturalFit.description: Measures how welcoming and navigable daily life is for a foreign remote worker. Not about tourist attractions — about real daily-life friction: can you get things done without speaking the local language? Are locals friendly? Is there bureaucratic hassle?
culturalFit.source: AI-analyzed (Claude Opus 4.6) — InterNations, NomadList, World Bank, travel advisories

budgetCategoriesPage.title: BUDGET CATEGORIES
budgetCategoriesPage.subtitle: What each cost category includes, how it's estimated, and where the data comes from.
budgetCategoriesPage.disclaimer: Budget category costs are generated by AI (Claude Opus 4.6, Anthropic) using data from Numbeo, Expatistan, and local price databases. Estimates represent typical monthly costs for a single digital nomad. Actual costs vary significantly based on lifestyle, location within the country, and current exchange rates. Always verify with local sources before making financial decisions.

housing.name: Housing
housing.description: Monthly rent for a furnished apartment suitable for remote work. Includes a private room or studio with reliable internet. Covers both major city and smaller city/regional options, with prices for 1-bedroom, 2-bedroom, and 3-bedroom apartments.
housing.source: AI-generated (Claude Opus 4.6) — Numbeo, Airbnb, local listings
housing.methodology: Based on median rental prices from Numbeo, cross-referenced with Airbnb monthly rates and local real estate listings. Adjusted for furnished vs unfurnished and nomad-typical neighborhoods.

groceries.name: Groceries
groceries.description: Monthly grocery spending for one person cooking at home regularly. Includes staples (rice, pasta, bread), fresh produce, dairy, meat/protein, and household essentials. Reflects local market prices, not imported specialty items.
groceries.source: AI-generated (Claude Opus 4.6) — Numbeo, local market data
groceries.methodology: Based on Numbeo grocery basket prices and local supermarket surveys. Assumes a balanced diet with a mix of local and common international ingredients.

dining.name: Dining
dining.description: Monthly spending on eating out — cafés, restaurants, and street food. Includes a mix of inexpensive local meals and mid-range restaurant visits typical of a nomad lifestyle. Covers coffee shop spending for working nomads.
dining.source: AI-generated (Claude Opus 4.6) — Numbeo, local restaurant data
dining.methodology: Calculated from Numbeo restaurant prices, averaging between inexpensive meal costs and mid-range dining. Includes an allowance for café visits with coffee and snacks.

transport.name: Transport
transport.description: Monthly local transportation costs including public transit passes, occasional ride-hailing (Uber/Bolt/local equivalent), and short-distance travel. Does not include intercity flights or long-distance travel.
transport.source: AI-generated (Claude Opus 4.6) — Numbeo, transit authority data
transport.methodology: Based on monthly public transport pass prices plus estimated ride-hailing costs for a typical urban nomad. Weighted toward public transit in cities with good infrastructure.

utilities.name: Utilities
utilities.description: Monthly utility bills including electricity, water, heating/cooling, garbage, and a home internet connection. Based on a standard apartment with moderate usage for a remote worker who needs reliable power and internet.
utilities.source: AI-generated (Claude Opus 4.6) — Numbeo, utility provider data
utilities.methodology: Derived from Numbeo utility estimates for a standard apartment, plus separate internet plan costs. Adjusted for climate (AC/heating needs) by region.

coworking.name: Coworking
coworking.description: Monthly cost of a hot desk or flexible membership at a coworking space. Covers spaces with reliable wifi, power, meeting rooms, and a professional work environment. Reflects the typical nomad coworking experience.
coworking.source: AI-generated (Claude Opus 4.6) — Coworker.com, NomadList, local listings
coworking.methodology: Based on average hot desk prices from Coworker.com and NomadList, cross-referenced with local coworking directories. Uses median pricing from established spaces in nomad-popular areas.

healthInsurance.name: Health Insurance
healthInsurance.description: Monthly cost of international health insurance suitable for a digital nomad — covering emergency care, hospitalization, and outpatient visits. Reflects typical plans from nomad-focused providers like SafetyWing, Genki, or World Nomads.
healthInsurance.source: AI-generated (Claude Opus 4.6) — SafetyWing, Genki, provider quotes
healthInsurance.methodology: Based on published rates from popular nomad health insurance providers. Represents a standard plan for a healthy adult aged 25-40 with global coverage excluding the US.
